exit_codes.3: operator decision or exact approval required

aliases.notice: Deprecated compatibility alias; migrate to workspace_run_profile before tool contract v2.

legacy_alias_tools.description: Deprecated compatibility alias; migrate to workspace_run_profile before tool contract v2. Use this compatibility alias only while migrating to workspace_run_profile.
legacy_alias_tools.name: workspace_verify
legacy_alias_tools.title: Verify workspace (deprecated alias)

tools.description:
Use this when helping with setup or debugging to read accepted/effective policy,
runtime package/generation/tool-surface health, connection-scoped negotiated client
capabilities, and policy changes still waiting for operator approval.
tools.name: config_inspect
tools.title: Inspect reviewed configuration

tools.description: Use this to idempotently request cancellation without marking terminal cancellation.
tools.name: operation_cancel
tools.title: Request operation cancellation

tools.description: Use this to list bounded durable operations by optional task/workspace scope and state.
tools.name: operation_list
tools.title: List durable operations

tools.description: Use this to inspect one exact durable operation and its bounded progress metadata.
tools.name: operation_status
tools.title: Read durable operation status

tools.description: Use this to inspect one exact reviewed commit with bounded file statistics and optional patch evidence.
tools.name: repo_commit_read
tools.title: Read committed change evidence

tools.description: Use this to compare two exact reviewed commits with merge-base, divergence, bounded files, and optional patch evidence.
tools.name: repo_compare
tools.title: Compare committed repository refs

tools.description: Use this before planning to inspect manifests, scripts, root files, and instruction previews.
tools.name: repo_context
tools.title: Read repository context

tools.description: Use this to list or filter native GitHub sub-issues and blocked-by relationships. Reads are bounded and cached briefly; pass fresh=true to bypass the cache.
tools.name: repo_issue_graph
tools.title: Query the GitHub-native ticket graph

tools.description: Use this to derive selectable tickets from one bounded GitHub-native graph snapshot, complete specs, closed blockers, active parents, WIP limits, and deterministic delivery order. Pass fresh=true to bypass the cache.
tools.name: repo_issue_next
tools.title: Select the next ready roadmap ticket

tools.description:
Use this when implementation requirements are defined by a GitHub issue. A recent
read of the same issue in this session may be served from a short-lived local cache
(marked `cache_hit: true`); pass `fresh=true` to force a live read, e.g. before acting
on a check or review that must not be stale.
tools.name: repo_issue_read
tools.title: Read GitHub issue

tools.description: Use this before implementing one ticket to combine its live GitHub contract, GitHub-native graph membership and metadata drift, and comment references without reconstructing prior chat. Evidence may be served from short-lived caches; pass `fresh=true` to force live reads.
tools.name: repo_issue_spec
tools.title: Read one ticket's specification and graph evidence

tools.description: Use this when choosing a repository or discovering its profiles and safety policy.
tools.name: repo_list
tools.title: List configured repositories

tools.description:
Use this to change one repository's command profiles, diagnostics, formatters,
relaxed-execution policy, or policy overrides through the reviewed immutable-generation
pipeline. Pass dry_run=true first to preview the capability delta. Restrictions and metadata-only
changes are applied immediately and hot reloaded; any capability expansion is only
stored as a pending change that the operator must approve in a terminal with
`rf config approve <change_id>` -- report that instruction and never claim an
expansion is active until config_inspect shows the new generation.

items.description: One complete profile definition supplied by the connected model.
items.title: ProfileDefinition

tools.name: repo_policy_apply
tools.title: Request gated repository policy change

tools.description:
Use this when reviewing an existing pull request, checks, commits, files, or reviews.
A recent read of the same pull request in this session may be served from a short-lived
local cache (marked `cache_hit: true`); pass `fresh=true` to force a live read before
acting on checks or reviews that must not be stale.
tools.name: repo_pr_read
tools.title: Read GitHub pull request

tools.description: Use this to read one UTF-8 file from an immutable reviewed repository snapshot.
tools.name: repo_read_file
tools.title: Read committed repository file

tools.description: Use this to read the same bounded line range from several files in one immutable snapshot.
tools.name: repo_read_files
tools.title: Read multiple committed repository files

tools.description: Use this when recent history or commit conventions are relevant to the task.
tools.name: repo_recent_commits
tools.title: Read recent commits

tools.description:
Use this to locate literal text in an immutable reviewed repository snapshot. Pass
context_lines (0-5) to also return that many surrounding lines on each side of a match
instead of a follow-up repo_read_file call; context lines are marked with `-` instead of
`:` after the path and line number, and still count toward max_results.
tools.name: repo_search
tools.title: Search committed repository code

tools.description: Use this when checking the source clone, remotes, branch state, and gh authentication.
tools.name: repo_status
tools.title: Inspect repository status

tools.description:
Use this when starting or resuming a task to assemble repository context, one
ticket's specification, workspace status, and recent commits in a single bounded call
instead of chaining repo_context, repo_issue_spec, workspace_status, and
repo_recent_commits. Pass issue_number and/or workspace_id to include those sections;
omitting either yields an explicit null, not an error. A supplied workspace_id must
belong to repo_id or the call fails closed. The ticket section reuses the same
short-lived local GitHub read cache as repo_issue_spec. Each section is independently
bounded and reports its own `truncated` flag, and the whole bundle is capped at 96 KB,
truncating recent_commits first, then ticket, then workspace, then repository last.
tools.name: repo_task_context
tools.title: Read bounded task-context bundle

tools.description: Use this to list files from an immutable reviewed repository snapshot without a workspace.
tools.name: repo_tree
tools.title: List committed repository files

tools.description:
Use this when diagnosing server or tool failures to read the redacted local
audit trail (source="audit"; filter by action name, failures only, or minimum
duration) or the managed runtime log tail (source="runtime"). Output is bounded
and never contains secrets or file bodies.
tools.name: runtime_logs_read
tools.title: Read bounded operational logs

tools.description: Use this for a git-style unified diff or OpenAI apply_patch envelope against an unchanged workspace; use an idempotency key for safe retries. Use workspace_edit for exact edits or workspace_write_file for full reviewed content. The response carries a fresh workspace_fingerprint and head_sha for the next locked call.
tools.name: workspace_apply_patch
tools.title: Apply validated patch

tools.description: Use this to compare the workspace base with configured local and latest remote base state.
tools.name: workspace_base_status
tools.title: Inspect workspace base freshness

tools.description: Use this after successful verification to stage and commit the exact verified tree.
tools.name: workspace_commit
tools.title: Commit verified changes

tools.description:
Use this before editing to create an isolated ai/* worktree; use an idempotency key for
retries. Create one workspace per issue; pass issue_ids only when several dependent
(stacked) issues are deliberately worked in this same workspace. issue_ids is
display-only metadata, not validated against any tracker.
tools.name: workspace_create
tools.title: Create isolated coding workspace

tools.description: Use this after push to create a draft PR with configured labels and reviewers.
tools.name: workspace_create_draft_pr
tools.title: Create draft pull request

tools.description: Use this after edits and before verification, commit, or publishing to review exact changes.
tools.name: workspace_diff
tools.title: Inspect workspace diff

tools.description: Use this for precise exact-text replacements across one or more files after validating each file's SHA and occurrence counts; use an idempotency key for safe retries. Pass one or more file entries, each with its own expected_sha256 and an ordered edits list (up to 20 edits per file, up to 20 files per call). All files are validated before anything is written, so the whole call is atomic -- if any file's SHA or occurrence count doesn't match, nothing is written. The response carries a fresh workspace_fingerprint and head_sha for the next locked call.

items.description: One file's ordered edits within a batched `workspace_edit` call.
One ordered exact-replacement entry within a `workspace_edit` file entry.
items.title: TextEdit
FileEdit

tools.name: workspace_edit
tools.title: Edit files

tools.description: Use this after editing to run one reviewed formatter over server-derived policy-allowed changed paths only; callers cannot provide argv, executables, environment values, working directories, or path lists.
tools.name: workspace_format_changed
tools.title: Format policy-allowed changed paths

tools.description: Use this to compare exact-base and current-workspace formatter findings without mutating repository or workspace state.
tools.name: workspace_hygiene_status
tools.title: Read baseline-aware workspace hygiene

tools.description:
Use this when resuming work or finding active RepoForge workspaces; each entry reports age,
dirty state, and linked issue_ids to help decide what to reuse or remove.
tools.name: workspace_list
tools.title: List coding workspaces

tools.description: Use this with an exact selector from workspace_pr_checks to inspect one Check Run.
tools.name: workspace_pr_check_details
tools.title: Read structured PR check details

tools.description: Use this to get compact pass, fail, pending, and skipped CI check buckets.
tools.name: workspace_pr_checks
tools.title: Read workspace PR checks

tools.description: Use this with a failed check selector to get redacted, bounded diagnostic evidence.
tools.name: workspace_pr_failure_evidence
tools.title: Read bounded PR failure evidence

tools.description: Use this to read draft state, mergeability, review decision, and rolled-up checks.
tools.name: workspace_pr_status
tools.title: Read workspace PR status

tools.description: Use this to start a durable exact-SHA check watch and return its operation reference.
tools.name: workspace_pr_watch
tools.title: Watch workspace PR checks

tools.description: Use this after commit to push the allowlisted ai/* branch without force.
tools.name: workspace_push
tools.title: Push AI branch

tools.description: Use this when reading one UTF-8 file and obtaining its optimistic-lock SHA-256.
tools.name: workspace_read_file
tools.title: Read workspace file

tools.description: Use this when the same bounded line range is needed from several related files.
tools.name: workspace_read_files
tools.title: Read multiple workspace files

tools.description: Use this to merge the exact reviewed base target without rebase, force push, or remote write; the response carries a fresh workspace_fingerprint for the next locked call.
tools.name: workspace_refresh
tools.title: Refresh workspace from reviewed base

tools.description: Use this to review one immutable merge preview against the latest configured remote base.
tools.name: workspace_refresh_preview
tools.title: Preview workspace base refresh

tools.description: Use this only after work is complete to remove a clean local worktree; remote data is untouched.
tools.name: workspace_remove
tools.title: Remove local workspace

tools.description: Use this to undo selected uncommitted tracked changes or remove selected untracked files; the response carries a fresh workspace_fingerprint and head_sha for the next locked call.
tools.name: workspace_restore_paths
tools.title: Restore selected workspace paths

tools.description: Use this only in a repository the owner has explicitly configured with execution_mode="relaxed", when no enrolled workspace_run_diagnostic template fits. argv is a bounded list (no shell, no shell metacharacters expanded) whose first element must be one of the repository's configured adhoc_runners. The result is evidence only: it is fully audited but never satisfies require_verification_before_commit -- run an enrolled verification profile on the exact tree immediately before workspace_commit. In a strict-mode repository this call returns a structured EXECUTION_MODE_STRICT error naming the enrolled-diagnostic and configuration alternatives instead of running anything. Set background=true for a long-running command; poll with operation_status.
tools.name: workspace_run_adhoc
tools.title: Run audited ad-hoc command

tools.description: Use this to run one typed repository-reviewed diagnostic; deterministic failures may be reused only when every reviewed binding is unchanged, and force_rerun=true bypasses that evidence reuse. The response carries fingerprint_after and head_sha for the next locked call when the fingerprint changed. Pass a single string or a bounded list of strings for a multi-value selector; use selector2 only when the diagnostic declares a second named placeholder. Call repo_task_context or repo_status first to see each enrolled diagnostic's selector schema (kind, character classes, max_values, expansion) before constructing a call.
tools.name: workspace_run_diagnostic
tools.title: Run reviewed workspace diagnostic

tools.description: Use this for an allowlisted setup, fix, build, or verification profile. Omit profile_name to run the repository-default verification profile. During the edit-test loop, prefer the quick profile or workspace_run_diagnostic; they are faster and cheaper to run repeatedly. Deterministic failures may be reused only when every reviewed binding is unchanged; set force_rerun=true to bypass that evidence reuse and execute commands again. Run the full or repository-default profile only once, right before workspace_commit. The response carries a fresh fingerprint and head_sha for the next locked call. Set background=true for a profile expected to run long: the call validates inputs, holds the workspace lock for the whole run, and returns an operation_id immediately -- poll it with operation_status (and cancel with operation_cancel if needed) instead of blocking this turn. The workspace stays locked to other mutations until the background run finishes.
tools.name: workspace_run_profile
tools.title: Run configured command profile

tools.description:
Use this when locating literal text in allowed workspace files; it is not a shell tool.
Pass context_lines (0-5) to also return that many surrounding lines on each side of a
match instead of a follow-up workspace_read_file call; context lines are marked with `-`
instead of `:` after the path and line number, and still count toward max_results.
tools.name: workspace_search
tools.title: Search workspace code

tools.description: Use this before writes to refresh HEAD, fingerprint, change budget, and verification state.
tools.name: workspace_status
tools.title: Inspect workspace status

tools.description: Use this when exploring tracked and untracked files allowed by repository policy.
tools.name: workspace_tree
tools.title: List workspace files

tools.description: Use this to update the existing workspace PR title or body; it does not mark it ready or merge.
tools.name: workspace_update_draft_pr
tools.title: Update draft pull request

tools.description: Use this to create or fully replace one UTF-8 file with optimistic locking; use an idempotency key for safe retries. The response carries a fresh workspace_fingerprint and head_sha for the next locked call, so workspace_status is not required in between.
tools.name: workspace_write_file
tools.title: Write complete file
